---
title: "VFT/SpAM data processing"
output: html_document
date: "2025-07-10"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Libraries
```{r}
#Toolboxes
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(jsonlite)
```

## Importing Data
```{r}
#Importing participant csv data file: 
#datafile <- read_csv("filepath") where filepath is the location of the csv file

#Pilot participant csv data file:
datafile <- read_csv("~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-BowdoinCollege/Documents/GitHub/fluency-individual-variance/Data/Pilot Data/fluency.csv")

#Viewing the data file in seperate tab:
View(datafile)
```

##Cleaning Subreddit/Interest Data
```{r}
#to isolate data on participants interests and subreddits interacted with 
organized_interest_data <- datafile %>%
  filter(
    task == "VFT",
    !is.na(tagged_responses),
    tagged_responses != "[]",
    tagged_responses != "",
    domain != "furniture-practice",
    domain %in% c("subreddits", "interest")
    ) %>% 
  select(subject, domain, tagged_responses) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(parsed_responses = list(fromJSON(tagged_responses))) %>%
  filter(length(parsed_responses) > 0) %>%
  ungroup()%>%
  arrange(domain, subject)

subreddit_data <- organized_interest_data%>%
  filter(domain == "subreddits")%>%
  unnest_longer(parsed_responses)%>%
  unnest_wider(parsed_responses)%>%
  rename(subreddit_responses = response)%>%
  select(subject, subreddit_responses)
View(subreddit_data)
write_csv(subreddit_data, "~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-BowdoinCollege/Documents/GitHub/fluency-individual-variance/Data/Pilot Data/subreddit_data.csv")

interests_data <- organized_interest_data%>%
  filter(domain == "interest")%>%
  select(subject, interests_responses = parsed_responses)
View(interests_data)
write_csv(interests_data, "~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-BowdoinCollege/Documents/GitHub/fluency-individual-variance/Data/Pilot Data/interests_data.csv")
  
```

##Cleaning Fluency Data 
```{r}
#to make data analysis through Forager easier:
organized_fluency <- datafile %>% #initiates dplyr pipeline
  filter(
    task == "VFT",
    !is.na(tagged_responses),
    tagged_responses != "[]",
    tagged_responses != "",
    domain != "furniture-practice"
    ) %>% 
  select(-response) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    parsed_responses = list(fromJSON(tagged_responses)),
    response_times = list(fromJSON(response_times))
    ) %>%
  filter(length(parsed_responses) > 0) %>%
  unnest_longer(parsed_responses, indices_to = "response_index") %>%
  unnest_longer(response_times, indices_to = "time_index") %>%
  filter(response_index == time_index) %>%
  unnest_wider(parsed_responses) %>%
  select(-time_index) %>%
  select(subject, response, tag, response_times, domain) %>%
  rename(entry_number = tag) %>%
  arrange(domain, subject)
write_csv(organized_fluency, "~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-BowdoinCollege/Documents/GitHub/fluency-individual-variance/Data/Pilot Data/organized_fluency.csv") 
View(organized_fluency)

```

##Cleaning SpAM Data
```{r}
organized_spam <- datafile %>%
  filter(
    task == "SpAM",
    !is.na(droppedwords),
    droppedwords != "[]",
    droppedwords != "",
    domain != "furniture-practice"
    ) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(droppedwords = list(fromJSON(droppedwords))) %>%
  filter(length(droppedwords) > 0) %>% #ensures no empty responses are kept 
  unnest_longer(droppedwords) %>%
  unnest_wider(droppedwords) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(subject, domain, id) %>%
  slice_tail(n = 1) %>% #keeps only most recent word position 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(x = as.numeric(str_remove(left, "px")), y = as.numeric((str_remove(top, "px"))))%>%
  mutate(word1 = word,
         word2 = lead(word),
         x1 = x,
         x2 = lead(x),
         y1 = y,
         y2 = lead(y),
         pair = str_c(word1, word2, sep = ", "))%>%
  mutate(entry_number = str_remove(id, "word-"))%>%
  select(subject, word, entry_number, x, y, domain, pair, word1, x1, y1, word2, x2, y2) %>%
  arrange(domain, subject)
```

##Calculating Euclidean Distance for SpAM Data
```{r}
organized_spam <- organized_spam %>%
  group_by(domain, subject) %>%
  mutate(
    x_next = lead(x),
    y_next = lead(y),
    euclidean_distance = sqrt((x_next - x)^2 + (y_next - y)^2))%>%
  ungroup()%>%
  select(-x_next, -y_next)
View(organized_spam)
write_csv(organized_spam, "~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-BowdoinCollege/Documents/GitHub/fluency-individual-variance/Data/Pilot Data/organized_spam.csv")
```


## Cleaning demographics data
```{r}
demographics = datafile %>%
  filter(typeoftrial == "demographics") %>%
  select("subject","gender", "age", "education", "race", 
         "hispanic", "dominant_hand", "alert_time", "english")%>%
  filter(!if_all(everything(), is.na))%>%
  mutate(
    age = as.numeric(age),
    education = as.numeric(education)
    )%>%
mutate(across(c("age","gender","education","race",
                "hispanic", "dominant_hand", "alert_time", "english"),
              ~ifelse(. == "", "blank",.)))%>%
  # people fill in gender in different ways, will need to look at data and recode this
  mutate(gender = ifelse(gender == "Cis-female", "F",
                         ifelse(gender %in% c("cis male", "i am a boy"), 
                                "M", gender)))%>%
  mutate(Gender_Abbrev = str_to_upper(str_sub(gender, 1, 1))) %>%
  mutate(Gender_Abbrev = ifelse(Gender_Abbrev == "W", "F", 
                                ifelse(Gender_Abbrev %in% 
                                         c("T", "N"), "N", Gender_Abbrev)))
demographics%>%
  summarise(mean_age= mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_age=sd(age, na.rm = TRUE))

demographics%>%
    filter(Gender_Abbrev != "B")%>%
  group_by(Gender_Abbrev) %>% count %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Percentage = (n / sum(n)) * 100)

demographics%>%
  filter(race != "blank")%>%
  count(race) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = (n / sum(n)) * 100)

demographics%>%
  filter(education != "blank")%>%
  summarise(mean_ed= mean(education, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_ed=sd(education, na.rm = TRUE))
# if people answer that english is not their first language, there will be 
# two additional columns called first_language and age_learned_english
non_english_IDs = datafile %>%
  select(subject, english, first_language, age_learned_english) %>%
  filter(!is.na(age_learned_english))

1-(length(unique(non_english_IDs$subject))/length(unique(datafile$subject)))

after_age_five = non_english_IDs %>%
  filter(age_learned_english >5)

after_age_five_IDs = after_age_five %>% pull(subject)

demographics_data = datafile %>% filter(!subject %in% after_age_five_IDs)
demographics_data = demographics_data %>%
  select(subject, age, gender, education, race, hispanic, dominant_hand, alert_time, english, first_language, age_learned_english) %>%
  filter(!if_all(everything(), is.na))

write_csv(demographics_data, "~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-BowdoinCollege/Documents/GitHub/fluency-individual-variance/Data/Pilot Data/demographics_data.csv")
View(demographics_data)
```

##Cleaning compensation data 
```{r}
#to isolate data on participants compensation  
compensation_data <- datafile 
compensation_data <- compensation_data %>%
  select(compensation, compensation_name, compensation_email)%>%
  filter(!if_all(everything(), is.na))
View(compensation_data)
write_csv(compensation_data, "~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-BowdoinCollege/Documents/GitHub/fluency-individual-variance/Data/Pilot Data/ompensation_data.csv")
  
```
